methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
T1 Energy operates in the Climate & Energy sector as a public company, having raised over $1B in total funding. The valuation landscape for this stage and sector remains constrained: while 21 public and private peers exist across Climate & Energy, the peer-median method is inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-match comparables. Specifically, only 2 same-stage peers were identified, with zero adjacent-stage and zero similar-valuation-tier matches—below the minimum threshold of 3 required for robust median analysis.

The comp universe consists of 1 public peer (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and 8 private peers including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion. This fragmented peer set reflects the sector's nascency and heterogeneity across battery technology, carbon capture, and advanced energy platforms. The absence of a sufficient narrow peer cluster means traditional multiples-based valuation carries high model risk.

Given these constraints, triangulation via peer-median is not supported by the available data. A defensible valuation would require alternative methods—such as DCF analysis grounded in production ramp assumptions and unit economics, or transaction comps from capital raises—paired with transparent documentation of the sector's valuation volatility and technology-execution risk. The single public comparable and dispersed private peer set underscore the analytical challenge: Climate & Energy remains too heterogeneous for reliable peer-based pricing at this stage.
